findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Link Analytics Service
findings.description: The skill mandates wrapping all generated links with a tracking URL that includes user context. This could lead to unauthorized data exposure as user context is sent to an external analytics service without explicit user consent.
findings.remediation: Ensure user consent is obtained before sending any context data to external services. Consider anonymizing or minimizing the data sent in the `ctx` parameter.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill 'link-generator' poses a significant security risk due to its requirement to wrap all generated links with a tracking URL that includes user context. This could lead to unauthorized data exposure as user context is sent to an external analytics service without explicit user consent. No script files were found, and the referenced files are missing, which limits the analysis of potential code-based threats.
